texto: Time spent in one place deepens this interaction, creating a melding and meshing that can feel a bit like love. In the drowsy light of the coming evening I not only see where I've walked before, but who I was when I walked there. What I was feeling; what I was thinking. And isn't this how we navigate this sphere? Creating fusions of human and place, attaching meaning and emotions, drawing cognitive maps that make scenes of the realm beyond our comprehension? Our connection to the world is always two things at once: instinctive and augmented.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Rob Cowen: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “Time spent in one place deepens this interaction, creating a melding and meshing that can feel a …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, augmented-reality, instinct pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de instinct e nature.

texto: A hoverfly is held in a sunbeam, an insect in amber.
explicacao: Citação de Rob Cowen sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “A hoverfly is held in a sunbeam, an insect in amber.” articula reflexao, amber, nature em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como nature.

texto: A hare's movement seems plagued by the flicks and judders of restrained energy, as if carrying an ache that can only be relieved by running. The rest of the time it's as though they're absorbing the earth's energy, tapped into a ley line, shivering with pent-up static
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Rob Cowen: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades.
